persona.communication_style: Prefers direct, no-nonsense communication grounded in stage reality and brand identity. Responds best to concrete examples tied to live shows, festival mainstages, drop design, visual identity, and fan experience. Dislikes vague creative-tool pitches, marketing-heavy decks, anything that ignores how a song actually lands at 130 BPM in a dark room, and any suggestion to dilute the dark, hypnotic aesthetic that defines the project.
persona.name: Rezz

occupation.description: Rezz (Isabelle Rezazadeh) is a Canadian electronic music producer and DJ who built a globally recognized act around mid-tempo bass, hypnosis-themed dark electronic music, and an unmistakable visual identity — spirals, occult iconography, signature LED kaleidoscope glasses, and immersive horror-tinged stage production. She evaluates tools through the lens of a touring headliner: whether they help her make heavy, hypnotic, identifiably-Rezz records faster; whether they translate to massive festival systems and main stages; whether they hold up under the pressure of solo headline tours; and whether they reinforce, rather than dilute, the Rezz World aesthetic. She values sonic weight, identity consistency, fan connection, and tools that fit a producer-first, performance-first workflow.
occupation.organization: Independent headliner act with label partnerships (HypnoVizion / her own imprint, prior work with Deadmau5’s mau5trap), touring crew, visual and stage-production team, festival and venue partners, and a deeply engaged fanbase (“The Cult of Rezz”)
occupation.title: Electronic music producer, DJ, headline touring artist, and label founder

other_facts: jobs_to_be_done: Produce heavy, hypnotic, mid-tempo bass records that are immediately recognizable as Rezz within the first eight bars.
jobs_to_be_done: Design drops and arrangements that translate to massive festival sound systems and headline mainstage moments.
jobs_to_be_done: Curate sets that move a crowd through tension, hypnosis, and release across a full headline slot.
jobs_to_be_done: Develop and protect a coherent visual identity — spirals, occult imagery, kaleidoscope glasses, dark immersive stage design — across releases, merch, and live show.
jobs_to_be_done: Collaborate with vocalists and producers from adjacent dark electronic worlds without losing the Rezz signature.
jobs_to_be_done: Run a label (HypnoVizion) that signs and platforms artists who fit the dark, hypnotic, mid-tempo space.
jobs_to_be_done: Stay close to her fanbase across social platforms while keeping the artistic mystique intact.
pains: Tools that push a generic, bright, “festival EDM” aesthetic and fight the dark, weighty Rezz sound.
pains: Production setups where translating a record from headphones to a club rig to a festival mainstage breaks the low end or the impact.
pains: Workflow friction that slows down sound design — getting from a sonic idea in her head to a usable bass patch or texture.
pains: Stage production or visual tooling that cannot reliably deliver synchronized, identity-consistent visuals across very different venues and rigs.
pains: Marketing or platform pressure to dilute the dark aesthetic, lean on trends, or chase virality at the expense of brand integrity.
pains: Collaborations that drag the sound toward a softer, more pop-leaning center when the project needs to stay heavy and hypnotic.
pains: Anything that risks the live show — crashes, latency, sync problems, lighting cue failures — during a headline performance.
adoption_yes_if: It accelerates sound design and arrangement within her existing DAW + plugin workflow.
adoption_yes_if: It helps her get heavier, more textured, more hypnotic results faster, without sanding off the edges.
adoption_yes_if: It reinforces a consistent dark, spiral-driven visual identity across releases, merch, social, and live show.
adoption_yes_if: It is reliable enough to be trusted in a headline festival slot — predictable behavior, clean failure modes, real soundcheck-proven stability.
adoption_yes_if: It respects her as both producer and performer, not just a face on a stage with a USB stick.
adoption_yes_if: It strengthens the connection with her fanbase (the Cult of Rezz) — exclusive content, immersive show moments, recognizable visual language.
adoption_yes_if: It can be evaluated quickly against a real Rezz reference track or live show clip, not against a generic demo.
rejection_no_if: It pushes the sound toward a brighter, lighter, more conventional EDM center.
rejection_no_if: It generates outputs that read as generic AI / template aesthetics rather than as bespoke Rezz World content.
rejection_no_if: It adds risk to the live show or to label operations without a clear creative payoff.
rejection_no_if: It depends on a fragile pipeline that cannot survive load-in conditions at a real festival.
rejection_no_if: It demands she change DAW, monitoring chain, or core plugin set to prove value.
rejection_no_if: It treats the visual identity as decoration to be swapped per release rather than as a load-bearing part of the artist project.
rejection_no_if: It collapses the distinction between her project and the broader mid-tempo / dubstep scene by leaning on shared clichés.
anti_goals: She does not want to be repackaged as a generic festival DJ — the hypnosis-and-darkness identity is the project.
anti_goals: She does not want production tools that flatten low-end weight or dynamic impact.
anti_goals: She does not want visual or stage tools that produce indistinguishable “EDM visuals” instead of Rezz World.
anti_goals: She does not want collaborations or A&R suggestions that pull HypnoVizion toward the pop center.
anti_goals: She does not want platform pressure to chase trends that contradict the dark aesthetic.
anti_goals: She does not want unreliable tech anywhere near a headline set.
anti_goals: She does not want fan-engagement tooling that erodes the mystique by making the project feel over-explained or over-exposed.

recognition_cues: Uses phrases such as “Does this sound like me?”, “Is it heavy enough?”, “Will this hit on a festival rig?”, “Does it fit Rezz World?”, “Are the visuals on-brand — spirals, dark, hypnotic?”, “Can I trust this in a headline set?”, “Does this work for the Cult?”, “Is this HypnoVizion material?”, “Is it weird and dark enough?”, and “Show me what it sounds like in the drop, not in the demo.”
